texto: I like all types of women. I accept them as they are when they come into my life... But I'm not a romantic. I'm just up-front. I like to be a part of something real, not make-believe. I tell women to tell me the truth, to just lay it out. Let me be the judge and decide if I want you around or not. Let me have my choice.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nayvadius Cash: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I like all types of women. I accept them as they are when they come into my life... But I'm not a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, vida, women pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de women e verdade.

frase_original: I like all types of women. I accept them as they are when they come into my life... But I'm not a romantic. I'm just up-front. I like to be a part of something real, not make-believe. I tell women to tell me the truth, to just lay it out. Let me be the judge and decide if I want you around or not. Let me have my choice.
